title: Velvety Golden Irish Potato Hash
description: A savory and cozy Irish dish featuring hojicha and katsuramuki-cut potatoes, perfect for a comforting meal. This kosher-friendly recipe is made using a tava-griddle.
instructions: Preheat the tava-griddle over medium heat.
In a large bowl, toss the katsuramuki-cut potatoes with hojicha powder, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Heat the vegetable oil on the preheated tava-griddle and add the diced onion, cooking until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the tava-griddle and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the coated potatoes to the tava-griddle, stirring to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Cook the potato mixture for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes are golden brown and crispy.
Use a spatula to shape the potato mixture into a flat, even layer on the tava-griddle.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, until the bottom of the potato hash is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the tava-griddle from the heat and sprinkle with chopped scallions, if desired.
Serve the Velvety Golden Irish Potato Hash hot, straight from the tava-griddle.
